_disclaimer: These are SIMULATED operational parameters for demo purposes. Facility identities and structural fields (address, level_of_care, payers, etc.) are real/public; availability, wait times, and decline behavior below are NOT real-time data and should never be presented as such.

intake_requirements_by_level._basis: Requirement sets are drawn from a fixed vocabulary matching common clinical intake practice per level of acuity (e.g., higher-acuity levels require current psych eval + risk assessment + medical clearance; lower-acuity outpatient levels require fewer). Assigned deterministically per facility (see generate_availability.py) so specific facilities differ from each other, supporting a readiness-diff demo.

accept_rate_ranges_by_level._basis: Ranges as specified in the task brief: inpatient 0.4-0.6, residential 0.5-0.7, IOP/OP 0.7-0.9. Extended proportionally to adjacent levels (crisis/ED slightly below inpatient given acuity; peer_respite/sobering near residential range given lower-barrier low-acuity access model).

wait_days_by_level._basis: SFDPH's own Residential Care and Treatment Working Group materials describe a step-down/residential bottleneck (patients clinically ready for a lower level of care awaiting placement); modeled here as a 1-4 week (7-28 day) wait band for residential_stepdown and residential_tx, shorter bands for higher-acuity levels that turn over faster, near-zero for outpatient/IOP which are least bed-constrained.

occupancy_basis_note: No accessible DataSF dataset directly reporting real-time psychiatric bed occupancy was found in this pass (see facilities_collection_notes.md); the 90-97% high-occupancy assumption commonly cited for urban psych inpatient units informs the narrow inpatient_psych/locked_subacute wait bands above rather than being stored as a literal field.
